title: Theogony: The Birth of the Gods
description: Hesiod's account of creation: from Chaos to the reign of Zeus, the genealogy of all the gods.
introduction: The Theogony is the oldest systematic account of creation in Western literature. Hesiod tells how the cosmos emerged from Chaos, how Earth (Gaia) gave birth to Heaven (Ouranos), how the Titans ruled and fell, and how Zeus established the present order of the gods. This is not merely mythology: it is the first attempt to explain the origin and structure of reality through a narrative of cosmic generations.

translation.original_text: ἤτοι μὲν πρώτιστα Χάος γένετ᾿· αὐτὰρ ἔπειτα Γαῖ᾿ εὐρύστερνος, πάντων ἕδος ἀσφαλὲς αἰεί, ἠδ᾿ Ἔρος, ὃς κάλλιστος ἐν ἀθανάτοισι θεοῖσι.
translation.text: In the beginning there came to be Chaos; and then broad-bosomed Earth, the ever-sure foundation of all, and Eros, the most beautiful among the immortal gods.

translation.original_text: Γαῖα δέ τοι πρῶτον μὲν ἐγείνατο ἶσον ἑαυτῇ Οὐρανὸν ἀστερόενθ᾿, ἵνα μιν περὶ πάντα καλύπτοι, ὄφρ᾿ εἴη μακάρεσσι θεοῖσ᾿ ἕδος ἀσφαλὲς αἰεί.
translation.text: Earth first bore starry Heaven, equal to herself, to cover her on every side, to be a secure seat forever for the blessed gods.

translation.original_text: Ῥείη δὲ δμηθεῖσα Κρόνῳ τέκε φαίδιμα τέκνα... τοὺς μὲν κατέπινε μέγας Κρόνος.
translation.text: Rhea, submitting to Kronos, bore him glorious children... but great Kronos swallowed them each as they came from the womb to her knees.
